title: Motion Picture Projectionists
description: Set up and operate motion picture projection and related sound reproduction equipment.

tasks.text: Monitor operations to ensure that standards for sound and image projection quality are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Start projectors and open shutters to project images onto screens.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Open and close facilities according to rules and schedules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate equipment to show films in a number of theaters simultaneously.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform regular maintenance tasks, such as rotating or replacing xenon bulbs, cleaning projectors and lenses, lubricating machinery, and keeping electrical contacts clean and tight.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up and adjust picture projectors and screens to achieve proper size, illumination, and focus of images, and proper volume and tone of sound.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect projection equipment prior to operation to ensure proper working order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Perform minor repairs, such as replacing worn sprockets, or notify maintenance personnel of the need for major repairs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up and inspect curtain and screen controls.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Coordinate equipment operation with presentation of supplemental material, such as music, oral commentaries, or sound effects.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect movie films to ensure that they are complete and in good condition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Remove full take-up reels and run film through rewinding machines to rewind projected films so they may be shown again.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install and connect auxiliary equipment, such as microphones, amplifiers, disc playback machines, and lights.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe projector operation to anticipate need to transfer operations from one projector to another.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare film inspection reports, attendance sheets, and log books.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Splice separate film reels, advertisements, and movie trailers together to form a feature-length presentation on one continuous reel.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
